shell.noCloudSync: Local first · optional Drive sync in Settings
shell.quickAddDesc: Record income, expense, or transfer data stored only on this device.

dashboard.title: Overview
dashboard.subtitle: Monthly spending, savings progress, and budget for {{month}}.
dashboard.emptyTitle: Start with a clean local workspace
dashboard.emptyDesc: Your workspace starts with no records. Add transactions with the quick-add button, or load demo data to explore the dashboard.
dashboard.noCategoryData: Expense categories will appear once transactions are added.
dashboard.insightEmptyTx: Add your first transaction to start seeing cash flow and spending patterns.
dashboard.insightLoadDemo: Load demo data from the dashboard if you want to evaluate the workspace first.
dashboard.insightTopCategory: {{name}} is your largest expense area at {{amount}}.
dashboard.insightRemainingBudget: You have {{amount}} left in the monthly expense budget.
dashboard.insightExpenseHigher: Expenses are {{pct}}% higher than last month.
dashboard.insightExpenseLower: Expenses are {{pct}}% lower than last month.

transactions.title: Transactions
transactions.subtitle: Search, filter, add, edit, delete, and export local transaction records.
transactions.emptyDesc: Transactions that match your filters will appear here. Start with one income or expense record to activate the dashboard.
transactions.dialogDesc: Transactions are saved to IndexedDB in this browser.
transactions.deleteDesc: Delete "{{note}}" for {{amount}}? This action cannot be undone.

budgets.title: Budgets
budgets.subtitle: Set monthly and category-level budgets, then track actual spend against them.
budgets.monthlySetupDesc: Budget amounts are nonnegative and saved by month.
budgets.setupInfoDesc: Set a monthly spending limit — overall and per category. Warnings appear at 80% and 100% usage to help you stay on track.
budgets.setupInfoRolloverDesc: Any unspent amount from the previous month is added to this month's budget, giving you extra room on months when you save.
budgets.budgetVsActualDesc: Warnings appear at 80%, 100%, and over budget.
budgets.emptyDesc: Save an overall monthly budget or category budgets to see usage here.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.subtitle: Review trends, category concentration, projected spending, and savings rate.
analytics.emptyDesc: Analytics activate after you add transactions or load demo data from the dashboard.
analytics.noNetWorthAccounts: No accounts are marked as 'Include in net worth'. Enable this in account settings.
analytics.trendInfoDesc: Income, expenses, and net savings tracked over the last 12 months.
analytics.trendInfoNet: Income minus expenses — positive means you saved money.

calendar.title: Calendar
calendar.subtitle: Daily spending heatmap — click any day to see transactions.
calendar.selectDay: Select a day to view transactions

accounts.title: Accounts
accounts.subtitle: Manage cash, bank, wallet, savings, investment, credit, and debt accounts.
accounts.emptyDesc: Accounts hold opening balances and transaction movement for net worth calculations.
accounts.deleteDesc: Connected transactions must be removed first. This action cannot be undone.
accounts.formCreditLimitHint: Optional. Enter the total credit or loan limit.
accounts.creditDebtExample: Example: SPayLater limit 20,000 already used 8,000 → initial balance -8,000, credit limit 20,000. To record a purchase use Expense on this account; to repay use Transfer from bank to this account.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Configure local preferences, categories, backup, restore, and data reset controls.
settings.privacyText: Finora stores financial data in IndexedDB inside this browser by default. If you enable Google Drive sync, Finora uploads a backup to your Google Drive app data using your Google account.
settings.backupRestoreDesc: Export complete JSON backups or transaction CSV files. Restore replaces local data.
settings.categoriesDesc: Add custom categories, edit names, or disable default categories.
settings.mrrRatesDesc: Update when banks announce rate changes. Used to calculate floating-rate mortgage payments.
settings.restoreDesc: Current local data will be replaced with the backup contents. This action cannot be undone.
settings.clearDesc: This permanently removes all transactions, budgets, accounts, and categories from this browser.

form.receiptRefHint: Receipt storage is planned for a later version.
form.duplicateWarning: This looks like a transaction already recorded on {{date}} {{time}}. Save again to add it anyway.

slip.title: Scan slip
slip.uploadHint: JPEG, PNG, WEBP — processed locally on this device
slip.reviewHint: Review the extracted values before applying. Category match may be inaccurate.
slip.errorFileType: Unsupported file — choose a JPEG, PNG, or WEBP image
slip.errorResolution: Image is too small to read (min 300×300 px)

budgetForm.noCategoryBudgets: No category budgets yet. Add one below.

goals.title: Savings Goals
goals.subtitle: Track progress toward financial targets — a house fund, emergency savings, or a big purchase.
goals.dialogDesc: Set a target and track your progress.
goals.emptyDesc: Create a goal to track progress toward a financial target.
goals.linkedAccountHint: Optional. Link to a savings account to track its balance as progress.
goals.deleteDesc: "{{name}}" and its contribution history will be permanently removed.

debts.title: Debts
debts.subtitle: Track loans, credit cards, and personal debts — and plan your payoff.
debts.dialogDesc: Record a debt with its interest rate and payment schedule.
debts.emptyDesc: Add a debt to track your balance, payoff date, and interest cost.
debts.linkedAccountHint: Optional. Link to a debt account to track balance automatically.
debts.paymentTooLow: Payment too low to cover interest
debts.revolvingNote: Projection assumes no new charges from today.
debts.floatingNote: Projection uses current rate. Update rate if MRR changes.
debts.presetPickerSub: Auto-fills typical rates — you can adjust any field.
debts.typeCash: EasyCash / บัตรกดเงินสด

recurring.title: Recurring
recurring.subtitle: Transactions that repeat automatically on a set schedule.
recurring.dialogDesc: Set a schedule and the transaction will be generated automatically.
recurring.pendingDesc: These recurring transactions are due. Review and confirm each one.
recurring.emptyDesc: Add recurring transactions for fixed expenses like rent, subscriptions, or salary.
recurring.autoGenerateHint: Generate transaction automatically when due. Turn off to confirm manually.
recurring.endDateHint: Optional. Leave blank to repeat indefinitely.
recurring.intervalHint: Number of days between each occurrence.
recurring.deleteDesc: "{{note}}" will stop generating. Past transactions are kept.

onboarding.title: Welcome to Finora
onboarding.desc: Your local-first personal finance workspace. Set a few preferences to get started — you can always change them later.

driveSync.title: Google Drive sync
driveSync.description: Optional sync stores a Finora backup in your Google Drive app data using the minimal Drive app-data permission.
driveSync.notConfigured: Google Drive sync is not configured for this build. Add VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID to enable it.
driveSync.statusOff: Google Drive sync is off. Local data stays in this browser.
driveSync.statusEnabledLastSynced: Google Drive sync enabled · last synced {{date}}
driveSync.statusSignInFailed: Google Drive sign-in failed. Try connecting again.
driveSync.statusConflictFound: Conflict found: this device and Google Drive both changed. Choose which copy to keep.
driveSync.statusRemoteNewer: Drive has newer data. Restore it or keep this device.
driveSync.statusCreated: Created a new Google Drive backup from this device.
driveSync.statusUploaded: Uploaded this device's latest data to Google Drive.
driveSync.statusRestoreFailed: Could not restore Google Drive backup.
driveSync.statusKeptDevice: Kept this device and overwrote the Google Drive backup.
driveSync.statusUploadFailed: Could not upload this device to Google Drive.
driveSync.statusDisconnected: Google Drive sync disconnected. Local data remains on this device.
driveSync.conflictDescription: Choose whether to replace this browser's local IndexedDB data with Drive data, or keep this device and overwrite Drive.
driveSync.shortcutNotConfiguredTitle: Google Drive sync is not configured
driveSync.shortcutConflictTitle: Open Settings to resolve the Google Drive sync conflict
driveSync.shortcutErrorTitle: Open Settings to review the Google Drive sync error
driveSync.shortcutReconnectTitle: Google Drive sync is enabled, but this browser session needs Google sign-in again
driveSync.shortcutSyncNeededTitle: Open Settings to sync this device safely

accounts.title: Account setup guide
accounts.intro: Learn how to set up each account type for accurate tracking.

cash.title: Cash
cash.description: Track physical cash in your wallet or at home.
cash.step1: Create an account with type Cash.
cash.step2: Set initial balance to the cash you currently have on hand.
cash.step3: Record cash purchases as Expense on this account.

bank.title: Bank account
bank.description: Track your bank account balance and transactions.
bank.step1: Create an account with type Bank.
bank.step2: Set initial balance to your current bank balance.
bank.step3: Record purchases as Expense. Receive salary as Income.

ewallet.title: E-wallet (TrueMoney, Rabbit LINE Pay, etc.)
ewallet.step1: Create an account with type E-wallet.
ewallet.step2: Set initial balance to the current wallet amount.
ewallet.step3: Top-ups: use Transfer from Bank to E-wallet.

creditCard.title: Credit card
creditCard.description: Track credit card spending and payments.
creditCard.step1: Create an account with type Credit card.
creditCard.step2: Set initial balance to negative outstanding balance, e.g. -5000 if you owe 5,000.
creditCard.step3: Purchases: record as Expense on the credit card account.
creditCard.step4: Payment: record as Transfer from Bank to Credit card.
creditCard.warning: Never record a credit card payment as an Expense — that double-counts your spending.

spaylater.title: SPayLater / BNPL / Debt account
spaylater.description: Track buy-now-pay-later balances like SPayLater, Aeon, or any personal loan.
spaylater.step1: Create an account with type Debt.
spaylater.step2: If you already owe 8,000, set initial balance to -8000 — it is debt.
spaylater.step3: If you have a credit limit of 20,000, set Credit limit to 20000.
spaylater.step4: Purchases: record as Expense on the SPayLater account.
spaylater.step5: Repayment: record as Transfer from Bank to SPayLater.
spaylater.warning: Do not record repayment as Expense — the spending already happened at purchase time.

investment.title: Investment account
investment.description: Track investments like stocks, mutual funds, or crypto.
investment.step1: Create an account with type Investment.
investment.step2: Set initial balance to the current portfolio value.
investment.step3: Deposits: use Transfer from Bank to Investment.
investment.step4: Returns or gains: record as Income on the Investment account.

netWorth.title: Include in net worth
netWorth.description: Control which accounts count toward your total net worth.
netWorth.step1: Enable for accounts you own: bank, savings, investments, e-wallets.
netWorth.step2: Debt and credit card accounts should also be included — they reduce net worth.
netWorth.step3: Disable for accounts you track on behalf of others.

transactions.title: Transaction recording guide
transactions.intro: Use the right transaction type to avoid double-counting.

cashPurchase.title: Cash purchase
cashPurchase.description: Record everyday purchases paid in cash.
cashPurchase.step3: Category: choose the relevant spending category.

creditPurchase.title: Credit card / SPayLater purchase
creditPurchase.description: Record purchases made with a credit card or BNPL service.
creditPurchase.step2: Account: select the Credit card or SPayLater account.
creditPurchase.step3: Do NOT record this on the Bank account — the bank has not paid yet.

creditRepayment.title: Credit card / SPayLater repayment
creditRepayment.description: Record a payment toward your credit card or BNPL balance.
creditRepayment.step3: To: the Credit card or SPayLater account.
creditRepayment.warning: Do not use Expense for repayment — the expense was recorded when you made the purchase.

ownTransfer.title: Transfer between own accounts
ownTransfer.description: Move money between your own accounts without creating a phantom expense.
ownTransfer.step4: Example: move salary to savings, or top up your e-wallet.

salaryIncome.title: Salary / income
salaryIncome.description: Record incoming money to your account.
salaryIncome.step2: Account: the bank account that receives the salary.
salaryIncome.step4: Amount: the amount actually received, not the gross salary.

debtInterestFee.title: Loan interest or fee
debtInterestFee.description: Interest and fees are real expenses, separate from principal repayment.
debtInterestFee.step2: Account: the debt or credit card account.
debtInterestFee.step4: Do not combine interest with the principal repayment transfer.

budgets.title: Budget guide
budgets.intro: Budgets help you plan and track monthly spending.

monthlyBudget.title: Monthly total budget
monthlyBudget.description: Set an overall spending cap for the entire month.
monthlyBudget.step3: Finora shows your spending vs. budget in real time.
monthlyBudget.step4: Tip: start with a round number and adjust after one month.

categoryBudget.title: Category budget
categoryBudget.description: Allocate a specific amount per spending category.
categoryBudget.step1: Add a category budget below the overall budget form.
categoryBudget.step2: Pick a category and enter the limit.
categoryBudget.step3: The bar turns red when you exceed the category limit.
categoryBudget.step4: Category budgets work independently from the overall budget.

rolloverBudget.title: Rollover budget
rolloverBudget.description: Carry unused budget from the previous month forward.
rolloverBudget.step1: Enable the rollover toggle on the monthly budget.
rolloverBudget.step2: Unused amount from last month adds to this month's cap.
rolloverBudget.step3: Useful for irregular expenses like car maintenance or travel.

overBudget.title: Over-budget state
overBudget.description: Know when you have exceeded your spending plan.
overBudget.step1: The budget bar turns red and shows Over when spending exceeds the limit.
overBudget.step2: Finora still records all transactions — no spending is blocked.
overBudget.step3: Review top categories on the dashboard to find where overspending happened.

recurring.title: Recurring transactions guide
recurring.intro: Automate regular income and expenses so you never have to enter them manually.

subscription.title: Monthly subscription
subscription.description: Record a fixed recurring expense like Netflix, Spotify, or gym membership.
subscription.step2: Set Type to Expense, enter the amount and category.
subscription.step3: Set Frequency to Monthly and choose a start date matching the billing date.
subscription.step4: Enable Auto-generate — the transaction will be created automatically each month.

salary.title: Monthly salary or income
salary.description: Automatically record income that arrives on a fixed date each month.
salary.step1: Click Add recurring, set Type to Income.
salary.step2: Enter the net amount received and select the bank account.
salary.step3: Set Frequency to Monthly with a start date matching your pay date.

customInterval.title: Custom interval (e.g. quarterly insurance)
customInterval.description: Use a custom interval for expenses that repeat every N days.
customInterval.step2: Enter the number of days between occurrences (e.g. 90 for quarterly).
customInterval.step3: Set the start date to the next due date.

manualConfirm.title: Manual confirmation (variable amounts)
manualConfirm.description: Use manual confirmation when the amount varies each time, like utilities.
manualConfirm.step1: Create the recurring transaction with Auto-generate turned off.
manualConfirm.step2: When it is due, it appears in the Pending confirmation section.
manualConfirm.step3: Click Confirm to review and generate the transaction for that period.

goals.title: Savings goals guide
goals.intro: Set targets, track progress, and stay motivated toward financial milestones.

emergencyFund.title: Emergency fund (manual tracking)
emergencyFund.description: Build a safety net by manually recording each time you set aside money.
emergencyFund.step1: Click Add goal and name it Emergency fund.
emergencyFund.step2: Set the target amount (e.g. 3–6 months of expenses).
emergencyFund.step3: Leave Linked account empty to use manual tracking.
emergencyFund.step4: Each time you save money, click Add contribution to update progress.

linkedAccount.title: Linked savings account
linkedAccount.description: Connect a goal to a savings or investment account — progress updates automatically as transactions are recorded.
linkedAccount.step1: Create a savings account in Accounts (type: Savings account).
linkedAccount.step2: Add a goal and select that account in Linked account.
linkedAccount.step3: Progress reflects the current account balance — no manual contributions needed.

deadline.title: Goal with a deadline
deadline.description: Add a target date to see how many days remain and stay on track.
deadline.step1: Set Target date when creating or editing a goal.
deadline.step2: The card shows days remaining, turning orange when the deadline is close.
deadline.step3: If the deadline passes without reaching the target, the card shows Overdue.

archive.title: Archiving a completed goal
archive.description: Keep your goal list tidy by archiving goals you have reached.
archive.step1: When progress reaches 100%, the goal shows a Completed badge automatically.
archive.step2: Click the Archive button on the goal card to move it out of the active list.
archive.step3: Toggle Show archived at any time to view or restore archived goals.

calendar.title: Calendar spending guide
calendar.intro: Use the calendar heatmap to spot heavy-spending days and review daily transactions.

heatmap.title: Reading the heatmap
heatmap.description: Each day cell is shaded by relative spending — darker means more spent.
heatmap.step1: Darker red cells = higher expense relative to other days in the month.
heatmap.step2: The shade is percentile-based, so one unusually large day will stand out.
heatmap.step3: Days from other months are shown dimmed for context.

dayDetail.title: Viewing day transactions
dayDetail.description: Click any day cell to see every transaction recorded on that date.
dayDetail.step1: Click a cell to open the detail panel (desktop) or bottom sheet (mobile).
dayDetail.step2: The panel shows income, expense, and transfer entries with category icons.
dayDetail.step3: Click the same cell again, or tap outside, to close the panel.

settings.title: Backup, sync, and data guide
settings.intro: Understand how Finora stores, backs up, and syncs your data.

localFirst.title: Local-first data
localFirst.description: Finora stores all finance data in your browser's IndexedDB — no account required.
localFirst.step1: Data stays on this device by default.
localFirst.step2: Clearing browser data or switching browsers loses local data.
localFirst.step3: Export a JSON backup regularly as a safety measure.

jsonBackup.title: JSON backup (export)
jsonBackup.description: Export a complete backup of all your data as a JSON file.
jsonBackup.step1: Settings → Backup and restore → Export JSON.
jsonBackup.step2: Save the file somewhere safe (cloud folder, external drive, etc.).
jsonBackup.step3: The file includes all transactions, accounts, budgets, and settings.

jsonRestore.title: JSON restore
jsonRestore.description: Restore a previous backup from a saved JSON file.
jsonRestore.step1: Settings → Backup and restore → Restore JSON.
jsonRestore.step3: Confirm the restore — this replaces all current local data.
jsonRestore.warning: Restore replaces everything in your local browser. Export a backup first if you want to keep your current data.

googleDriveSync.title: Google Drive sync
googleDriveSync.description: Automatically sync your data to Google Drive's hidden app data.
googleDriveSync.step1: Settings → Google Drive sync → Connect Google Drive.
googleDriveSync.step2: Finora stores a backup in your Drive's hidden app data (not visible in Drive Files).
googleDriveSync.step3: Sync runs automatically when you open the app.
googleDriveSync.step4: You can trigger a manual sync with the Sync now button.

syncConflict.title: Sync conflict resolution
syncConflict.description: When this device and Drive both have changes, you must choose which to keep.
syncConflict.step1: Use Drive data — replaces this device's data with the Drive backup.
syncConflict.step2: Keep this device — uploads local data and overwrites the Drive backup.
syncConflict.step3: Disconnecting stops sync, but your local data stays on this device.
syncConflict.warning: Always export a JSON backup before resolving a conflict if you are unsure which copy is more up to date.

debts.title: Debt tracking guide
debts.intro: Track balances, project payoff dates, and find the fastest path to being debt-free.

presets.title: Debt type presets
presets.description: Choosing a type auto-fills the typical interest rate and payment structure — adjust any field to match your actual contract.
presets.bnpl: 0% if paid in full · installment: 15% (Unicorn) or 25% (Money Capital) p.a.

addDebt.title: Adding your first debt
addDebt.description: Use the preset picker to auto-fill typical rates — then adjust any field to match your actual contract.
addDebt.step1: Click Add debt and pick the debt type that matches yours (e.g. Credit card, Car loan).
addDebt.step2: The preset fills in a typical interest rate and payment structure — verify against your statement or contract.
addDebt.step3: Enter the original loan amount as Principal and your monthly payment.
addDebt.step4: Save — Finora projects your payoff date and total interest remaining immediately.

linkedAccount.title: Linking to a debt account
linkedAccount.description: If you already track a debt as an Account (e.g. credit card, SPayLater), link it here so the remaining balance updates automatically from your transactions.
linkedAccount.step1: Create a Debt-type account in Accounts if you haven't already, and record repayments as Transfer transactions.
linkedAccount.step2: When adding or editing a debt, select that account in Linked account.
linkedAccount.step3: Finora reads the account's live balance — no need to update the debt manually each month.

floatingRate.title: Floating-rate mortgage (MRR-based)
floatingRate.description: Mortgages tied to a bank's MRR change when the bank adjusts its rate. Set up the spread once — update when the bank announces a rate change.
floatingRate.step1: Choose Mortgage and set Rate type to Floating (MRR-based).
floatingRate.step2: Select your bank from the dropdown — Finora fills in the current MRR.
floatingRate.step3: Enter your spread (e.g. -1.5 means MRR − 1.5%). The effective APR is computed for you.
floatingRate.step4: When your bank changes its MRR, edit the debt and re-select the bank to get the updated rate.
floatingRate.warning: The projection uses the current rate. A rate change will shift your payoff date — re-open the debt to update after any MRR announcement.

whatIf.title: What-if: paying extra
whatIf.description: See exactly how much interest you save and how many months earlier you become debt-free by paying more than the minimum.
whatIf.step1: On a debt card, click What if — a dialog shows your current payoff date and total interest.
whatIf.step2: Enter an extra monthly amount. The payoff date updates instantly.
whatIf.step3: Use the global Extra per month field at the top of the page to simulate paying more across all debts at once.

strategy.title: Avalanche vs. Snowball
strategy.description: Two popular strategies to tackle multiple debts — Finora shows both so you can choose the approach that fits you.
strategy.step1: Avalanche: put extra money toward the highest-rate debt first. Minimises total interest paid — the mathematically optimal approach.
strategy.step2: Snowball: put extra money toward the smallest balance first. You clear debts sooner and may feel more motivated.
strategy.step3: The Strategy comparison section shows the debt-free date and total interest for each — pick the one that motivates you most.
